fields.description:
# Invalidate downstream plan reviews after upstream plan-material changes

## Problem
A plan-review attestation can remain valid after a direct child or prerequisite changes its plan. In a large epic or dependency chain, an agent can therefore claim or close work using assumptions that no longer match the plans it relies on. The existing material fingerprint protects only the reviewed ticket itself; it does not bind the reviewed plan to the current material of related tickets.

## Outcome
Every new plan review records signed plan-material pins for the ticket's direct children and direct prerequisites. A pin binds the related ticket's canonical ID, its role, and its current narrow material fingerprint. When enforcement is enabled, material drift in a pinned ticket invalidates the downstream attestation. Propagation is deliberately natural rather than eager: an upstream change invalidates its direct dependent; farther downstream tickets invalidate only if the intermediate ticket adapts materially.

## Why this approach
Signed pins extend the existing computed-on-read attestation-validity model: the downstream review cryptographically records exactly what related plan material it relied on, while later reads compare that evidence without writing graph-wide invalidation events. This avoids tickets-branch write amplification, introduces no new storage primitive, and permits legacy attestations and older readers to continue by ignoring additive metadata. Natural propagation also preserves the causal boundary: a downstream plan is reconsidered only when an intermediate plan actually changes.

## Settled design

### Material and relationships
- Material remains the current narrow definition: description and acceptance criteria, file impact, and direct-child IDs.
- Attestation validity is not propagated. A related ticket's review can be invalid without affecting downstream tickets when its material did not change.
- Direct prerequisites are the union of outgoing depends_on targets and inbound blocks sources. Stored relation types are unchanged; aliases and short IDs are resolved before use.
- Parent/child review behavior already exists and is not changed by this epic. Children participate only in material pinning.

### Signed plan-material pins
- The final deterministic manifest order is: plan-review, ticket, material, model, runner, blocking, advisory, review-phase, optional priority-floor, rebar-version, optional regver, optional disabled_builtins, optional refreshed-from, optional verified-at-sha, zero or more plan-material-pin lines sorted lexicographically by role (child before prerequisite) then lexicographically by canonical ID, then zero or more dep lines sorted by path. plan-material-pin syntax is plan-material-pin: <role> <canonical-ticket-id> <material-fingerprint>; roles are exactly child or prerequisite. A target in both roles has two pins; duplicate graph edges within one role collapse. Add manifest_review_phase(), manifest_priority_floor(), and manifest_pins() parsers beside existing manifest parsers; every lifecycle consumer uses them.
- A target is resolvable and readable only when its stored reference canonicalizes to exactly one existing reducer-readable ticket state containing the same canonical ticket_id. Archived states and empty descriptions are readable because the narrow fingerprint remains computable. Missing/deleted targets, ambiguous or malformed references, reducer errors, or canonical-ID mismatch are unresolved/unreadable and stop review before any LLM call. At later validity checks, deletion of a previously pinned target is missing-target drift.
- For a readable pinned prerequisite, consistent and finding are complete evaluations. indeterminate is used only when specialized model evaluation, output parsing, or attribution cannot complete. It never asserts a blocking incompatibility, but complete prerequisite coverage is required for certification, so any indeterminate record yields an unsigned INDETERMINATE review rather than signed PASS. Oversized input likewise yields unsigned too-large remediation.
- The phase story owns one unified pre-sign transaction covering subject material, plan_review_phase, relationship membership, and related fingerprints. The concrete ticket_store_revision is the tickets tracker worktree HEAD SHA after ensure_fresh; every supported write appends, stages, and commits while holding the same global write lock. A dirty or unmerged tracker is an error, never a comparable revision. Outside the lock, read revision-before, re-collect all dimensions, then read revision-after; retry unless equal. Acquire the global lock and require HEAD still equals revision-after, compare the fresh snapshot with the pre-LLM snapshot, then append plus commit the signature under that lock. On revision change retry outside-lock collection at most three times; exhaustion returns retryable unsigned. LockTimeout during the pre-sign commit has the same retryable unsigned disposition. This detects new children or inbound blockers globally without reducing tickets under the lock.
- Pins are automatic signed attestation metadata. There is no new CLI or MCP operation and no first-order ticket field.
- Backward verification does not reconstruct a manifest from recognized fields. The DSSE/op-cert record stores and authenticates the complete manifest list byte-for-byte; an older verifier authenticates that stored list including unknown pin lines, then its field-specific parsers ignore lines they do not consume. Therefore unknown additive lines do not alter signature reproduction.
- New parsers raise one ManifestFormatError on duplicate, malformed, unknown-enum, missing-token, extra-token, or invalid numeric lines. manifest_pins() returns [] only when no pin lines exist; manifest_priority_floor() returns None only when no floor line exists; manifest_review_phase() returns planning only when the phase line is absent; a priority-floor line without a review-phase line raises ManifestFormatError. In the plan-review attestation branch, compute_plan_review_validity parses all three fields together inside one narrow ManifestFormatError catch and returns malformed/invalid; the catch excludes reopen, material, dependency-hash, and other non-format work, and the completion-verifier branch does not parse plan-review-only phase, floor, or pins.
- Legacy attestations without pins remain valid. No migration or eager backfill is performed; the next ordinary re-review adds pins.
- Archived tickets remain readable. A pinned target that has been deleted or is missing makes the pin stale and requires relationship repair followed by re-review.

### Enforcement and rollout
- verify.enforce_plan_material_pins defaults to false. Pin drift remains visible but advisory while disabled; valid remains true. compute_plan_review_validity preserves its top-level {valid, reason, verdict} contract and adds health: DerivedPlanReviewHealth. That nested contract has pin_status exactly current, stale-pin-drift, stale-pin-missing, malformed-pin, or legacy-unpinned; phase_status exactly compatible, incompatible, or malformed; enforced boolean; and targets: list[TargetPinDetail]. TargetPinDetail is exactly {canonical_id: string, role: child|prerequisite, pinned_fingerprint: string, current_fingerprint: string|null, pin_status: current|stale-pin-drift|stale-pin-missing|malformed-pin}; legacy-unpinned has an empty targets list. Disabling enforcement does not hide drift: pin_status stays stale while enforced=false and top-level valid remains true. Malformed manifest syntax remains the shared malformed review state.
- Re-enabling enforcement immediately applies existing pin-aware attestations. It never rewrites pins, while legacy unpinned attestations continue to pass. Pin lines are additive manifest metadata: readers predating this feature ignore unknown lines and therefore treat the attestation like a legacy unpinned review. Rollback has two explicit paths. To disable only the opt-in gates, set enforce_plan_material_pins=false and require_plan_review_for_close=false; this does not change the fixed execution-review floor. To remove the 0.80 execution-review behavior, first disable both gates, then deploy the preceding code version; a runtime floor flag is intentionally not added. No stored attestation must be rewritten. Snapshots containing plan_review_phase remain rollback-safe because process_snapshot restores unknown compiled-state keys and compaction drops only its explicit strip set, so an older reducer must pass plan_review_phase through unchanged on re-compaction rather than strip it. Compatibility tests run old reduction plus re-compaction against a phase-bearing snapshot, then roll forward and prove the exact phase value survives without bootstrap re-derivation or corruption.
- Pin validity uses one implementation wherever an attestation is already being evaluated: claim, close, attestation reuse, drift refresh, re-sign, audit, and derived health. The pin flag does not independently activate claim or close gates. At close, pin drift can block only when both require_plan_review_for_close and enforce_plan_material_pins are enabled; otherwise observed drift is advisory.
- New reviews emit pins in every project regardless of enforcement configuration. The rebar project enables enforcement.

### Execution reviews and close gate
- Ticket review phase is an internal reducer-maintained plan_review_phase projection with values planning or execution, carried in compiled state and compaction snapshots; the distinct name avoids the existing prompt execution_mode (agentic/single_turn). Only the winning/normal branch of process_status updates plan_review_phase, mirroring _fold_claimed_session and _fold_close_class: a winning committed STATUS event targeting in_progress sets execution, one targeting open sets planning, and other targets preserve the value, including force transitions. Losing fork events never update the phase. Reviews read this O(1) projection and never scan raw history. Example: open -> in_progress -> blocked remains execution; blocked -> open resets planning.
- For a pre-feature SNAPSHOT missing plan_review_phase, bootstrap planning when its compiled status is open or idea and execution for any other status. A reducer-cache version bump makes the rule apply on first post-upgrade reduction; the derived value is then carried forward idempotently in later snapshots. A later open event always resets it.
- The signed manifest line review-phase: has exactly planning or execution. Execution reviews also carry priority-floor: 0.80. A syntactically valid floor is numeric in [0.0, 1.0]; an execution attestation is compatible only at >= 0.80. Absence of review-phase is the legacy planning value; missing, nonnumeric, or range-invalid execution floors are malformed.
- Planning mode accepts only planning. Execution mode accepts planning (the stricter full review) or execution with priority-floor numerically at least 0.80; lower or malformed execution floors are incompatible.
- During execution, normal Pass 3 blocking-threshold resolution applies effective_block_threshold = max(registry.threshold_for(criterion), 0.80) to blocking criteria. This does not replace or alter novelty_priority_floor or completion_priority_floor. Advisory-only criteria stay advisory, deterministic structural failures stay blocking, and lower-priority findings remain visible. The 0.80 value is a fixed protocol risk policy for already-started work, not an empirically claimed optimum or project config. The numeric manifest field is evolution-safe: a future required floor compares numerically, so raising policy makes older execution attestations require re-review while lowering it accepts attestations carrying the older higher floor; no manifest-format migration is required.
- verify.require_plan_review_for_close defaults to false. When enabled, task, story, and epic close operations require a current certified plan review. Bugs, session logs, code reviews, identities, and idea-to-closed transitions remain exempt.
- Close performs no LLM review. A failed gate directs the agent to run review-plan separately.
- Close checks signature, compatible phase, the ticket's own material, relationship pins, and other non-code attestation validity, but intentionally ignores file dependency hashes and whole-HEAD freshness because implementation normally changes impacted files.
- The existing force-close with a non-empty reason bypasses completion and close-time plan-review gates with an audit record. Structural child-closure rules remain non-bypassable.
- Review snapshots phase, material, and pins before the LLM call and rechecks them before signing. Close prechecks before completion verification and rechecks under the store write lock before writing STATUS.

### Prerequisite consistency review
- A specialized criterion runs deterministically when direct prerequisites exist. General finder and verifier calls never receive prerequisite material. The compiled state map is used only for canonical resolution, pinning, and specialized-criterion input construction; the specialized criterion runs in separate calls with isolated prompts. One preload supplies inbound blocks discovery without per-target store reductions.
- The criterion receives the subject plan and the current narrow material of each prerequisite; existing parent/child criteria and prompts are unchanged.
- Multiple prerequisite evaluations may share a call when whole prerequisite blocks fit within existing context headroom. Batching is an optimization only: each evaluation asks whether the subject is consistent with one prerequisite.
- Pass 1 returns exactly one PrerequisiteCoverageRecord per canonical prerequisite. Its schema is {prerequisite_id: canonical string, disposition: consistent|finding|indeterminate, findings: list[Finding], reason_code?: evaluation-error|output-invalid|attribution-invalid, detail?: string}. consistent requires empty findings and omits reason_code; finding requires one or more findings attributed to that same prerequisite_id and omits reason_code; indeterminate requires empty findings plus one reason_code and may carry non-authoritative diagnostic detail. Model/provider failure maps to evaluation-error; missing, duplicate, unknown-ID, parse-invalid, or schema-invalid output maps to output-invalid; failed or unprovable canonical attribution maps to attribution-invalid. Every indeterminate is unsigned and cannot block.
- Prerequisite candidate findings add optional prerequisite_id to the existing finding schema. The shared index-keyed Pass 2 verification schema gains optional prerequisite_attribution_valid = yes|no|na; non-prerequisite findings use na. Pass 2 receives the isolated ID map and sets yes only when the indexed original finding names exactly its in-bin canonical ID. Pass 3 preserves prerequisite_id on upheld findings, drops attribution=no candidates as unsupported, and maps missing/malformed verification to the unsigned INDETERMINATE coverage record. No parallel decide pipeline is introduced.
- Reuse sizing.container_budget(), sizing.is_context_limit_error(), and the existing model ladder. Pre-pack whole prerequisites by the budget. A provider context-limit error splits the failing stable-order bin into contiguous halves and retries recursively. A singleton escalates through models_at_or_above(); if it still exceeds the largest window, emit too-large remediation. Non-context failures become unsigned INDETERMINATE and are never misclassified as size failures.
- Packed behavior uses the existing whole-child bin-packing pattern and is release-gated against a one-prerequisite-per-call gold baseline: packed recall must be no lower, packed false acceptance no higher, every prerequisite must receive a record, and attribution errors must be zero. Tail-item fixtures are mandatory. Failure keeps the smaller-bin or one-per-call fallback rather than shipping degraded packing.

### Health and documentation
- Derived pin and review-phase health appears in detailed CLI show, MCP ticket detail, audit output, close errors, and the web ticket-review UI. Ordinary list output remains unchanged.
- Health is derived at read time; no warning event is persisted. When enforcement is off, the UI labels drift advisory.
- plan-material-pin is documented in advanced attestation/gate documentation, not basic user documentation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every new passing review signs all direct-child pins.
- [ ] Every new passing review signs all normalized prerequisite pins.
- [ ] Pin identities use canonical IDs plus the exact role enum.
- [ ] Pre-sign re-collection aborts after any concurrent relationship or fingerprint change.
- [ ] Legacy unpinned attestations remain valid.
- [ ] Older readers ignore additive pin lines without invalidating the signature.
- [ ] Enabled pin enforcement invalidates every named consumer after related material drift.
- [ ] Disabled pin enforcement reports drift without invalidating the attestation.
- [ ] The plan_review_phase projection survives compaction.
- [ ] Losing fork STATUS events never alter plan_review_phase.
- [ ] Pre-feature snapshots bootstrap phase from compiled status on first post-upgrade reduction.
- [ ] Entry into in_progress establishes execution phase.
- [ ] Entry into open resets planning phase.
- [ ] Execution reviews apply the fixed 0.80 priority-floor.
- [ ] Sub-floor execution findings remain visible.
- [ ] Enabled close enforcement requires a separately earned compatible review.
- [ ] Code freshness alone never invalidates the close-profile review.
- [ ] Every readable prerequisite receives one three-disposition coverage record.
- [ ] Every upheld prerequisite finding carries one verified canonical ID.
- [ ] Packed prerequisite evaluation matches the one-per-call fidelity contract.
- [ ] All detailed review surfaces consume the shared derived-health contract.
- [ ] Rebar enables both opt-in enforcement flags while reusable defaults remain disabled.

## Not in scope
- Consolidating or migrating depends_on and blocks storage.
- Changing existing parent/child review criteria or injecting relationship context into every reviewer.
- Eager graph-wide or transitive invalidation events.
- Persisted invalidation warnings or a new user-facing pin command.
- Backfilling or rewriting existing attestations.
